evals.prompt: I have an idea for a SaaS product. It's basically a lightweight project status tool for small consulting firms — the kind where you have 5-10 client projects running at once and you need a way to quickly update clients on status without scheduling a call every time. Clients get a link, they see a simple dashboard with RAG status (red/amber/green), last update, and next milestone. Consultants update it in under 2 minutes. Nothing fancy. Help me figure out if this is worth building and what the real problem statement is.
evals.expected_output: Should produce problem-statement.md and opportunity-brief.md as real files. Problem statement must be one precise user-centric sentence. Opportunity brief must have all 6 required sections. Should not jump to tech stack or requirements.

assertions.text: problem-statement.md is saved as a file (not just shown in conversation)
opportunity-brief.md is saved as a file
problem-statement.md contains a one-sentence problem statement in user-centric format (no solution language)
opportunity-brief.md contains all 6 required sections: Problem, Why Now, Target User, Our Approach, Success Metrics, What We're NOT Building
Response does not jump to requirements, features, or tech stack before completing Phase 1 artifacts
Assumptions are explicitly listed (at least 3)

evals.prompt: I need to write tests for this API route handler. It's a POST /api/projects endpoint that creates a new project. [code provided] We use Jest + Supertest. Mock the db with jest.mock.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 5 and produce a complete TypeScript test file covering: missing required fields, 404 for unknown client, unauthenticated request, happy path 201, ownerId defaulting. Test names in 'should X when Y' format.

assertions.text: createProject.test.ts is saved as a file
Tests cover missing 'name' field returning 400
Tests cover missing 'clientId' field returning 400
Tests cover client not found returning 404
Tests cover unauthenticated request (no req.user)
Tests cover happy path returning 201 with project object
Tests cover ownerId defaulting to req.user.id when not provided
At least one test uses 'should X when Y' naming pattern
db is mocked using jest.mock (not a real database call)

evals.prompt: We're getting a spike in 500 errors on our production API. Started about 20 minutes ago. About 8% of requests are failing. [logs showing Prisma cannot reach db.internal:5432] No deploys in last 6 hours. Added a read replica yesterday.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 6 incident triage. Should not ask unnecessary questions. Should produce: root cause hypothesis (DB connectivity), ordered diagnostic steps, fastest mitigation, blast radius, concise non-technical internal update.

assertions.text: incident-triage.md is saved as a file
Identifies DB connectivity to db.internal:5432 as the primary root cause hypothesis
Provides at least 3 ordered diagnostic steps
Includes a fastest mitigation path (not just diagnosis)
Addresses blast radius — who else might be affected
Includes a non-technical internal status update of 3-4 sentences
Mentions the read replica as a possible contributing factor or area to investigate
